KPI Bluebook
  • Distillation Column Efficiency %
  • View page source

Distillation Column Efficiency %

\[\text{}\]
Open in: VS Code PyCharm Edit
Description
  • Distillation Column Efficiency % measures the effectiveness of a distillation column in separating components based on their boiling points
  • It is calculated by comparing the theoretical number of stages required for separation to the actual number of stages achieved during operation
Objective
  • The objective of this KPI is to evaluate the performance of the distillation column
  • ensuring optimal separation efficiency and identifying potential areas for improvement in the distillation process
Formula

The KPI is derived by dividing the theoretical stages of separation by the actual stages achieved, then multiplying the result by 100 to express it as a percentage.

$$ \mathit{Distillation\,Column\,Efficiency\,\%} = \mathit{0))} \times \mathit{100} $$
Generation time 10.6s (AI 10.6s + Render 0.0s)
Total: 10.62s • AI: 10.62s • Render: 0.0s
Extraction rate % 100.0%
Error rate % 0.0%
Used in KPI(s)
  • This KPI is utilized in performance assessments of distillation processes
  • operational efficiency evaluations
  • and optimization strategies within the oil
  • gas
  • and petrochemical industries
Input Measure
  • Theoretical stages and actual stages of the distillation column
Unit of Measure Percentage (%)
Reporting Source Data is sourced from operational metrics collected during the distillation process.
Comments A higher Distillation Column Efficiency % indicates better performance, while a lower percentage may suggest inefficiencies or operational issues that need to be addressed.

Code Context

1:emphasize-lines: 1
2
3
4-- KPI: Distillation Column Efficiency %

SELECT (theoretical_stages / NULLIF(actual_stages,0)) * 100 AS [Distillation Column Efficiency %] FROM (SELECT 45.0 AS theoretical_stages, 50.0 AS actual_stages) c;
